mechanism_check: The architecture correctly implements the initial part of the hypothesis mechanism. The WideResNet-50 backbone is instantiated, properly adapted for 1-channel grayscale input via weight averaging, and frozen. It extracts features from layers 2 and 3 as specified. However, the provided code snippet is heavily truncated, missing the full implementation of the LoSAIN module, FAISS retrieval, patch merging, EVT fitting, and anomaly scoring logic.
spec_alignment: The visible design accurately aligns with the spec fields (A) and (B). The grayscale adaptation and feature layer extraction match exactly. Because the backbone is frozen, the learnable parameter count is 0, which complies with the ≤ 2.0M parameter budget. The rest of the spec requirements (C through I) must be validated in the completed code.
metric_evaluation_path: The evaluation path cannot be verified due to the truncated code snippet. The full implementation must include a rigorous evaluation script calculating False Positive Rate on Normal Images, False Alarm per Image, and Score Stability metrics, alongside latency profiling to ensure inference time ≤ 120s.
constraints_compliance: The parameter budget constraint is met by freezing the backbone. The requirement for normal-only/training-light is conceptually met by relying on a FAISS reference bank rather than backpropagation. To meet the 16GB GPU memory constraint for 6559x6559 images, the final code must implement memory-efficient patch batching and strictly handle FAISS index memory allocation.

issues.description: The provided model.py is severely truncated, cutting off inside the LoSAINModule signature. The full implementation of FAISS retrieval, LoSAIN, anomaly scoring, EVT calibration, and Gaussian patch merging is missing.
issues.design_suggestion: Ensure the complete implementation of all pipeline stages (C-I) is provided. Specifically, implement the LoSAIN forward pass using the mean and std extracted from the FAISS top-k reference list.

issues.description: Performing FAISS retrieval and LoSAIN normalization independently for 729 patches per image could become a major computational bottleneck if not vectorized/batched properly.
issues.design_suggestion: Ensure that query features for the 729 patches are batched before querying the FAISS index, and that the LoSAIN module can accept batched query features and batched reference statistics.

issues.description: Patch Merging (overlap-tile stitching) for a 6559x6559 heatmap on a single 16GB GPU could cause Out-Of-Memory (OOM) errors if intermediate dense tensors are kept on the GPU.
issues.design_suggestion: Perform the Gaussian-weighted blending on CPU, or process blending in smaller spatial chunks if GPU acceleration is strictly necessary.

missing_components: FAISS IVF-PQ indexing and batched retrieval logic
unnecessary_complexity: Using IVF-PQ (approximate nearest neighbor) for a relatively small bank (10k-50k features) might be overkill and introduce quantization errors. Flat L2 (Exact NN) in FAISS is extremely fast for <= 50k items and avoids clustering/quantization overhead.
bottlenecks: Repeated CPU-GPU memory transfers if FAISS is run on CPU while feature extraction and LoSAIN are on GPU.
Accumulating the full 6559x6559 float32 anomaly map in memory before downscaling or thresholding.
overall_comment: The foundational design for the feature extractor and the conceptual framework are highly aligned with the hypothesis. However, the truncated code prevents a full review of the critical LoSAIN and EVT logic. Special care must be taken regarding memory management and batching during the implementation of the remaining pipeline steps to strictly adhere to the latency and hardware constraints.
